~~~
<!-- PlutoStaticHTML.Begin -->
<!--
    # This information is used for caching.
    [PlutoStaticHTML.State]
    input_sha = "06d2831df5dffefaf39a22b3fcf6000583ebef3f0d62c82ccbafdfa7c7a2f928"
    julia_version = "1.12.4"
-->

<div class="markdown"><h1 id="Solving-the-damped-pendulum-equations-of-motion">Solving the damped pendulum equations of motion</h1><p><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lIiBIkiQWEU &quot;Damped pendulum numerical solution part 1 - click to watch&quot;"><img alt="Damped pendulum numerical solution part 1" src="https://img.youtube.com/vi/lIiBIkiQWEU/0.jpg"/></a></p><p><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EOZNMCmjY28 &quot;Damped pendulum numerical solution part 2 - click to watch&quot;"><img alt="Damped pendulum numerical solution part 2" src="https://img.youtube.com/vi/EOZNMCmjY28/0.jpg"/></a></p><p>The equations of motion for this damped oscillating system are as follows, </p><ol><li><p class="tex">$$m\ddot{x} + \frac{mL}{2}\ddot{\theta}\cos\theta - \frac{mL}{2}\dot{\theta}^2\sin\theta + b\dot{x} + kx = 0$$</p></li><li><p class="tex">$$\frac{mL}{2}\ddot{x}\cos\theta + \frac{mL^2}{3}\ddot{\theta} + \frac{mgL}{2}\sin\theta = 0$$</p></li></ol></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>using ModelingToolkit, OrdinaryDiffEq, Plots, Latexify </code></pre>



<div class="markdown"><p>In order to solve for the motion of the pendulum, we assign values to the spring stiffness, damper, gravity, mass, and length of the compound pendulum system. </p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>begin
    k = 2 # N/m
    b = 5 # kg/s
    g = 9.81 # m/s/s
    m = 0.1 # kg mass of pendulum bar
    L = 0.9 # m length of pendulum bar
end</code></pre>
<pre class="code-output documenter-example-output" id="var-b">0.9</pre>


<div class="markdown"><h2 id="Coupled-equations-of-motion">Coupled equations of motion</h2><p>In general, the motion of one degree of freedom can affect other degrees of freedom. This creates <em>coupled differential equations</em>. Each integration step requires a solution to the set of equations to find the acceleration of the degrees of freedom. </p><p>In this case, we create 2 functions:</p><ol><li><p>a mass-matrix function, <code>M(theta)</code> that changes with angle</p></li><li><p>a <code>pendulum!</code> function that updates the changes in our 'state' variable derivatives, <code>dy</code><span class="tex">\(=[\dot{x},~\dot{\theta},~\ddot{x},~\ddot{\theta}]\)</span></p></li></ol><blockquote><ul><li><p>of note for the <code>pendulum!</code> function is that the <code>!</code> is a <a href="https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/manual/functions/#man-argument-passing">Julia convention</a> that the first argument is being mutated</p></li><li><p><a href="https://discourse.julialang.org/t/assignment-and-mutation/19119">variable mutation</a> is subtly different than "assigning" a value. </p></li><li><p>why mutate vs assign??: There are pros/cons to each. Here it makes sense since the value of <code>dy</code> is used, but not saved anywhere so its better to update with current values. </p></li></ul></blockquote></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>M(theta) = [m m*L/2*cos(theta);
    m*L/2*cos(theta) m*L^2/3]</code></pre>
<pre class="code-output documenter-example-output" id="var-M">M (generic function with 1 method)</pre>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>function pendulum!(dy, y, params, time)
    # physical parameters 
    b, k, m, L, g = params

    x, theta, dx, dtheta = y
    
    dy[1:2] .= [dx, dtheta]
    
    rhs = [-b*dx-k*x+m*L*dtheta^2*sin(theta);
          -m*g*L/2*sin(theta)]

    dy[3:4] .= M(theta)\rhs
    return nothing
end</code></pre>
<pre class="code-output documenter-example-output" id="var-pendulum!">pendulum! (generic function with 1 method)</pre>


<div class="markdown"><h2 id="Compare-damping-terms-and-response">Compare damping terms and response</h2><p>Here, we try 4 different damping terms to see what effect it has on the motion of the pendulum. Counterintuitively, the <span class="tex">\(b=1000~kg/s\)</span> has the smallest effect on pendulum motion. </p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>begin
# plots_array = []
P = plot()
for b in [0.5, 1, 1.5, 100]
    initial_state = [0.0, pi/3, 0.0, 0.0]
    time_span = (0.0, 10.0)
    params = (b, k, m, L, g)
    problem = ODEProblem(pendulum!, initial_state, time_span, params)
    solution = solve(problem, Tsit5())
    plot!(P, solution.t, 
          solution[2,:]*180/pi, 
         label = "b = $b kg/s",
         linewidth = 4)
    
end
P
end</code></pre>
<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"/>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>begin
    initial_state = [0.0, pi/3, 0.0, 0.0]
    time_span = (0.0, 10.0)
    params = (0.5, k, m, L, g)
    problem = ODEProblem(pendulum!, initial_state, time_span, params)
    solution = solve(problem, Tsit5());
end</code></pre>

<pre class="code-output documenter-example-output" id="var-damper_wall_x">frame_data (generic function with 1 method)</pre>


<pre class="code-output documenter-example-output" id="var-plot_frame">plot_frame (generic function with 1 method)</pre>


<div class="markdown"><h2 id="Visualize-the-motion">Visualize the motion</h2><p>The <code>frame_data</code> and <code>plot_frame</code> functions were generated with copilot help, </p><blockquote><p>Julia Assistant input, </p><p><code>I want to create julia animations that make this image with a spring, damper, and compound pendulum based on vectors for x position and angle theta</code></p></blockquote><p>The resulting animation shows the damped motion with the <code>solution</code> created above. </p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>@gif for i in 1:length(solution)
    plot_frame(solution[1,:], solution[2,:], i)
end</code></pre>
<img src="data:image/gif;base64,"/>
<div class='manifest-versions'>
<p>Built with Julia 1.12.4 and</p>
Latexify 0.16.10<br>
ModelingToolkit 11.10.0<br>
OrdinaryDiffEq 6.108.0<br>
Plots 1.41.5
</div>

<!-- PlutoStaticHTML.End -->
~~~
